# Tallyard

> Free, transparent calculators and buying guides for home improvement. Every formula public, every source cited. No signup, no email capture, no affiliate links, no lead generation.

Tallyard is a home improvement calculator site built by Ash K. It covers 45 calculators, 3 in-depth buying guides, and 6 project planners across 8 project categories. The site targets US homeowners planning DIY or contractor projects.

## Editorial policy

- Every calculator shows its full formula step by step
- Coverage rates and sizing factors come from manufacturer specs, building codes (IRC, IPC, NEC), and industry standards (ACCA, NADRA, APA, TCNA)
- Results round up to practical purchase quantities
- No affiliate links, no contractor lead capture, no email gates
- Corrections within 48 hours of report
- Author is not a licensed contractor; formulas derive from published standards, cited on each page

## Calculators (45 total)

For complete methodology on every calculator, see /llms-full.txt.

Each entry is: name — formula — source standard — URL.

### Drywall

- Drywall: sheets = ⌈((wall area + ceiling area) ÷ sheet area) × (1 + waste)⌉. Source: US Gypsum Company — Drywall Installation Guide. [Drywall](/drywall-calculator)

### Electrical

- Drain Pipe: total DFU = sum(fixtures × DFU rating); pipe size per IPC/UPC tables (toilet line ≥ 3\. Source: IPC 2021 — Drainage Fixture Units. [Drain Pipe](/drain-pipe-calculator)
- Extension Cord: smallest AWG where ampacity at given length ≥ load; voltage drop = 2 × I × R × L / 1000. Source: OSHA — Extension Cord Safety. [Extension Cord](/extension-cord-calculator)
- Wire Size: smallest AWG where ampacity ≥ load AND voltage drop ≤ limit (VD = 2 × I × R × L / 1000). Source: NEC Table 310.16 — Conductor Ampacity. [Wire Size](/wire-size-calculator)

### Flooring

- Backsplash: tiles = ⌈((linear ft × height − outlets − window) × 1.15) ÷ tile area⌉. Source: Tile Council of North America. [Backsplash](/backsplash-calculator)
- Countertop: area = (linear ft × depth) + island + waste; linear edge = main front + island perimeter. Source: NKBA — Kitchen Planning Guidelines. [Countertop](/countertop-calculator)
- Flooring: boxes = ⌈(area × (1 + waste)) ÷ sq ft per box⌉. Source: National Wood Flooring Association (NWFA). [Flooring](/flooring-calculator)
- Grout: lb = (tile perimeter × joint × thickness × 150) ÷ (tile area × 12) × floor area × (1 + waste). Source: Mapei — Grout Coverage Chart. [Grout](/grout-calculator)
- Kitchen Cabinet: base ft = perimeter − appliances; upper ft = perimeter × 70-75%; cabinets = ⌈ft × 12 ÷ 24⌉. Source: NKBA — Kitchen Planning Guidelines. [Kitchen Cabinet](/kitchen-cabinet-calculator)
- Shower Tile: tiles = ⌈((3 walls + niche + floor) × 1.15) ÷ tile area⌉. Source: Tile Council of North America — Wet Area Installation. [Shower Tile](/shower-tile-calculator)
- Tile: tiles = ⌈(area ÷ tile size) × (1 + waste)⌉, boxes = ⌈tiles ÷ per box⌉. Source: Tile Council of North America — Installation Handbook. [Tile](/tile-calculator)
- Vanity: recommended = largest standard width (18-84\. Source: IRC 2021 Chapter 3 — Plumbing Fixtures. [Vanity](/vanity-calculator)

### HVAC

- BTU: BTU = area × 20 × height × climate × sun + occupancy adjustment. Source: Energy Star — Room Air Conditioners. [BTU](/btu-calculator)
- Heat Pump: BTU = sqft × climate factor × insulation × ceiling × windows; tons = BTU ÷ 12,000. Source: ACCA Manual J — Residential Load Calculation. [Heat Pump](/heat-pump-calculator)
- Water Heater: tank: size = peak hour demand ÷ 1.3; tankless: GPM = peak simultaneous, BTU = GPM × temp rise × 500. Source: US DOE — Water Heater Sizing. [Water Heater](/water-heater-calculator)

### Insulation

- Insulation: bags = ⌈(area × (1 + waste)) ÷ bag coverage⌉ at R-value for climate zone. Source: US DOE — Insulation R-Value Recommendations. [Insulation](/insulation-calculator)

### Landscaping

- Deck: boards = ⌈area ÷ board coverage × 1.1 ÷ 16⌉; joists = ⌈length ÷ spacing⌉ + 1. Source: IRC 2021 — Deck Design Provisions. [Deck](/deck-calculator)
- Fence: posts = ⌈length ÷ spacing⌉ + 1 + gates × 2 + corners, pickets = length × 12 ÷ picket width. Source: This Old House — Fence Installation Guide. [Fence](/fence-calculator)
- Gravel: volume = area × depth, weight = volume × density (tons per yd³ or tonnes per m³). Source: Aggregate Research — Gravel Density Reference. [Gravel](/gravel-calculator)
- Mulch: volume = area × depth, converted to cubic yards or meters (or bags). Source: University of Maryland Extension — Mulch Basics. [Mulch](/mulch-calculator)
- Paver: pavers = ⌈area ÷ paver area × (1 + pattern waste)⌉; includes base gravel and bedding sand. Source: ICPI — Interlocking Concrete Pavement Institute. [Paver](/paver-calculator)
- Pool Chlorine: dose = (volume ÷ 10,000) × ppm needed × product factor (oz per 10k gal per ppm). Source: Pool & Hot Tub Alliance (PHTA). [Pool Chlorine](/pool-chlorine-calculator)
- Rainwater Harvesting: gallons = roof ft² × rainfall inches × 0.623 × efficiency. Source: American Rainwater Catchment Systems Association. [Rainwater Harvesting](/rainwater-calculator)
- Sod: pieces = ⌈(area × (1 + shape waste)) ÷ coverage per piece⌉. Source: Turfgrass Producers International. [Sod](/sod-calculator)
- Topsoil: volume = area × depth, converted to cubic yards/meters (plus tons and bags). Source: Penn State Extension — Soil and Composting. [Topsoil](/topsoil-calculator)

### Lumber

- Lumber: board feet = (thickness × width × length_ft) ÷ 12, per board × quantity with waste. Source: American Lumber Standards Committee. [Lumber](/lumber-calculator)
- Shed: floor + walls + roof framing + sheathing for any shed footprint and wall height. Source: IRC 2021 — Detached Accessory Structures. [Shed](/shed-calculator)
- Stair: risers = round(total rise ÷ ideal riser); treads = risers − 1; stringer = √(rise² + run²). Source: IRC 2021 Chapter 3 — Stairways. [Stair](/stair-calculator)
- Stud Spacing: studs = ⌈length × 12 ÷ spacing⌉ + 1 + corners × 2 + doors × 6 + windows × 8. Source: IRC 2021 — Wood Wall Framing. [Stud Spacing](/stud-spacing-calculator)
- Window Sizing: openable = glass × window type factor; egress = 5.7 ft² min + 20\. Source: IRC 2021 Section R310 — Emergency Escape and Rescue Openings. [Window Sizing](/window-sizing-calculator)

### Masonry

- Asphalt: tons = area × thickness × density × (1 + waste), where density = 145 lb/ft³. Source: National Asphalt Pavement Association (NAPA). [Asphalt](/asphalt-calculator)
- Brick: bricks = area × (144 ÷ ((brick L + joint) × (brick H + joint))) × wythe × (1 + waste). Source: Brick Industry Association — Technical Notes. [Brick](/brick-calculator)
- Chimney: masonry fireplace: flue area = opening area ÷ 10 (or ÷ 8 if < 15 ft tall); wood stove: match outlet diameter. Source: IRC 2021 Chapter 10 — Chimneys and Fireplaces. [Chimney](/chimney-calculator)
- Concrete: volume = area × thickness × (1 + waste), converted to cubic yards or meters. Source: Portland Cement Association — Concrete Basics. [Concrete](/concrete-calculator)
- Mortar: bags = (wall_area_ft² × bricks_per_ft² × joint_volume_per_brick) ÷ coverage_per_bag. Source: Brick Industry Association — Technical Note 8 (Mortar for Brick Masonry). [Mortar](/mortar-calculator)
- Rebar: bars = ⌈dimension ÷ spacing⌉ + 1 per direction; total = (long × long length) + (trans × trans length) + perimeter. Source: CRSI — Concrete Reinforcing Steel Institute. [Rebar](/rebar-calculator)

### Paint

- Paint: paint = (perimeter × height − doors − windows) × coats ÷ coverage. Source: Benjamin Moore — Paint Coverage Chart. [Paint](/paint-calculator)
- Wallpaper: rolls = ⌈((perimeter × height − openings) × (1 + pattern waste)) ÷ roll coverage⌉. Source: Wallcoverings Association — Estimating Guide. [Wallpaper](/wallpaper-calculator)

### Roofing

- Attic Ventilation: NFVA = attic area ÷ ratio (1:300 or 1:150), split 50/50 between intake and exhaust. Source: FHA Minimum Property Standards. [Attic Ventilation](/attic-ventilation-calculator)
- Garage Door: door ≤ 10ft = single, ≤ 18ft = double; opener HP by area × material weight factor. Source: DASMA — Door & Access Systems Manufacturers Association. [Garage Door](/garage-door-calculator)
- Gutter: gutter = roof edge ft; downspouts = ⌈effective area ÷ (600-1200 ft² per DS)⌉; size = 5\. Source: SMACNA — Architectural Sheet Metal Manual. [Gutter](/gutter-calculator)
- Roofing: bundles = ⌈(footprint × slope × (1 + waste)) ÷ square size × 3⌉ + ridge caps. Source: GAF — Roofing Calculator Guide. [Roofing](/roofing-calculator)
- Siding: squares = ((perimeter × height + gables − openings) × (1 + waste)) ÷ 100. Source: James Hardie — Installation Guide. [Siding](/siding-calculator)
- Snow Load: load (psf) = snow depth × density + ice × 57; compare vs design load (code-specified). Source: ASCE 7 — Minimum Design Loads. [Snow Load](/snow-load-calculator)

### Solar

- Solar Panel: panels = ⌈(daily kWh ÷ (sun hours × efficiency) × 1000) ÷ panel watts⌉. Source: NREL PVWatts Calculator. [Solar Panel](/solar-calculator)

## Buying guides

- Vinyl vs fiber cement siding: 30-year total-cost-of-ownership comparison. Fiber cement lasts 50+ years vs vinyl 20-40 and wins on 30-year cost and fire rating; vinyl wins on upfront cost. URL: /guides/vinyl-vs-fiber-cement-siding
- Composite vs pressure-treated vs cedar decking: 20-year cost analysis. Pressure-treated is cheapest installed but most expensive over 20 years because its surface boards need replacing around year 15. URL: /guides/composite-vs-pressure-treated-vs-cedar-deck
- Heat pump vs furnace + AC: climate-zone operating-cost comparison with federal tax-credit math. Heat pumps save \$300-500/yr in zones 1-4, break even in zone 5, and lose to gas furnaces in zones 6-7. URL: /guides/heat-pump-vs-furnace

## Project planners

Multi-calculator tools that generate a complete material list from one set of inputs.

- Remodel a bathroom (5 calculators): /planner/remodel-a-bathroom
- Build a deck (4 calculators): /planner/build-a-deck
- Replace a roof (3 calculators): /planner/replace-a-roof
- Build a patio (3 calculators): /planner/build-a-patio
- Install a fence (2 calculators): /planner/install-a-fence
- Paint a room (2 calculators): /planner/paint-a-room

## Cost guides

Material + labor cost ranges (2026 US averages): deck, roof replacement, fence, house painting, flooring, bathroom remodel, concrete, siding, solar, HVAC replacement. Index: /guides
